title: comparo/v1
description: A comparo/v1 object - one of Environment, Request, Schema, Instance, Matrix, DiffProfile, AssertionProfile, ExecutionProfile, or Project.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: Object metadata. Every kind except ``Project`` declares an ``id``.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the TUI and reports.

id.description: Stable, unique identity other objects reference. Omitted only on Project.

description.description: Free-text explanation of what this object is for.

tags.description: Labels for the manifest's default selection and matrix filtering.

baseUrl.description: Base URL every request endpoint is joined to.

timeout.description: Per-request timeout budget for this environment.

secrets.description: Declared secrets by name; the value is a source ($env/$file/$literal/$from).

variables.description: Named string values referenced as ${NAME} or {$var: NAME}.

headers.description: Headers applied to every request unless it overrides them.

health.description: Readiness probes run by `comparo health` and the TUI.

auth.description: Default Basic/Bearer auth applied to every request unless it sets its own.

Duration.title: Duration
Duration.description: A split timeout budget; an omitted field falls back to a built-in default.

connect.description: Time allowed to establish the connection.

read.description: Time allowed to read the full response.

streamIdle.description: Max gap allowed between two streamed chunks.

streamMax.description: Hard cap on the total duration of a streamed response.

Header.title: Header
Header.description: A single HTTP header. ``value`` may be a literal or a reference hole.

value.description: The value: a literal, a ${...} string, or a directive hole.

description.description: Free-text note (kept from an OpenAPI import).

required.description: Whether the header is required (documentation, from an import).

type.description: A hint for the value's type (from an OpenAPI import).

HealthCheck.title: HealthCheck
HealthCheck.description: A request used to probe an environment's readiness.

method.description: HTTP method for the readiness probe.

headers.description: Extra headers to send with the probe.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

response.description: The expected response — schema, diff profile, assertions.

matrix.description: Matrices ({$use: matrix.x}) this request is expanded across.

HttpRequest.title: HttpRequest
HttpRequest.description: The outbound HTTP request a ``Request`` object describes.

endpoint.description: Path (joined to baseUrl); may carry ${...} holes.

query.description: Query parameters as a name→value map.

headers.description: A list of Header objects or a {Name: value} map ({$val: id} injects a shared set).

body.description: The request body (shape depends on bodyType).

bodyType.description: How the body is encoded: json (default), form, or raw.

auth.description: Basic/Bearer auth for this request; overrides the environment default.

cookies.description: Cookies to send, as a name→value map.

Response.title: Response
Response.description: The expected response of a ``Request`` object.

schema.description: A Schema to validate the body against ($use: schema.x or inline JSON Schema).

diff.description: A DiffProfile ($use/inline/list) overriding the project default comparison.

streaming.description: Whether the response is streamed (SSE / chunked).

assert.description: AssertionProfiles ($use/inline/list) checked on the response — the `assert` key.

Schema.title: Schema
Schema.description: A JSON Schema used for structural validation.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

Instance.title: Instance
Instance.description: A reusable value injected by reference.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

InstanceSpec.title: InstanceSpec
InstanceSpec.description: The body of an ``Instance`` object — a single reusable value.

value.description: The reusable value injected wherever {$val: this-id} appears.

Matrix.title: Matrix
Matrix.description: A set of parameter cases a request runs against.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

MatrixSpec.title: MatrixSpec
MatrixSpec.description: The body of a ``Matrix`` object — a list of atomic cases.

target.description: Where each case is injected: request.query, request.body, or request.path.

values.description: The cases; the cartesian product across matrices is expanded.

mode.description: merge updates the target container with each case; replace substitutes it.

createPath.description: Create missing intermediate objects along the target path.

DiffProfile.title: DiffProfile
DiffProfile.description: How two responses are compared, per JSON path.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

default.description: The comparison mode for any path not matched by a rule.

rules.description: Path-scoped overrides of the default mode.

DiffRule.title: DiffRule
DiffRule.description: One path-scoped comparison rule inside a ``DiffProfile``.

path.description: The JSON path this rule scopes ($ is the body root; $headers.x, $status too).

schema.description: A JSON Schema for `mode: shape` structural comparison.

arrayLength.description: For arrays: compare length exactly or tolerantly.

AssertionProfile.title: AssertionProfile
AssertionProfile.description: A composable set of response assertions, attached to a request or execution.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

AssertionProfileSpec.title: AssertionProfileSpec
AssertionProfileSpec.description: The body of an ``AssertionProfile`` — composable response assertions.

include.description: Other AssertionProfiles ({$use: id}) composed in first.

AssertionRule.title: AssertionRule
AssertionRule.description: One response assertion: a target, an operator, and an expected value.

target.description: What to assert on: $status, $headers.x, or a body path $.a.b.

op.description: The operator: equals, lt/lte/gt/gte, between, oneOf, exists, contains, schema.

value.description: The expected value for the operator.

severity.description: error fails the gate; warn is advisory.

ExecutionProfile.title: ExecutionProfile
ExecutionProfile.description: A named run plan: what to run, which environments, and which checks.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

ExecutionProfileSpec.title: ExecutionProfileSpec
ExecutionProfileSpec.description: The body of an ``ExecutionProfile`` — what to run, where, and which checks.

select.description: Which requests to run (by tag and/or id).

matrix.description: Per-matrix scoping, keyed by matrix id.

profiles.description: Diff/assert profile overrides for this execution.

report.description: Report overrides for this execution (same shape as Project.report).

ExecutionSelect.title: ExecutionSelect
ExecutionSelect.description: Which requests an execution runs — by tag and/or explicit id.

tags.description: Run requests carrying any of these tags.

MatrixScope.title: MatrixScope
MatrixScope.description: Per-execution matrix customization: which cases to run, add, or drop.

override.description: Add extra cases for this execution.

ExecutionEnvironments.title: ExecutionEnvironments
ExecutionEnvironments.description: The environments to run against; a ``candidate`` enables the diff.

baseline.description: The baseline environment name or id.

candidate.description: The candidate environment; its presence enables the diff.

diff.description: Whether to diff baseline vs candidate.

ExecutionProfiles.title: ExecutionProfiles
ExecutionProfiles.description:
The ``profiles`` block of an ExecutionProfile — its diff / assert overrides.

Typed (not ``Any``) so a mistyped key like ``asert`` is a hard load error
rather than a silently-ignored profile.

diff.description: A DiffProfile ($use/inline/list) applied for this execution.

assert.description: AssertionProfiles ($use/inline/list) applied for this execution — the `assert` key.

kind.description: The object kind — selects the shape of `spec`.

metadata.description: Name and optional description/tags (no id on Project).

ProjectSpec.title: ProjectSpec
ProjectSpec.description:
The body of the root ``Project`` manifest.

Interiors are strict structs so a mistyped key (``defualt``, ``concurency``)
is a hard load error, not a silently-ignored setting. ``diff`` stays ``Any``
because it holds ``$use``/inline profile holes; ``plugins`` is gated by a
dedicated load error.

data.description: Directory the object YAML lives in, relative to the manifest.

environments.description: The default environment and named diff pairs.

selection.description: Default request selection for headless run/diff.

redaction.description: Redaction options (the value floor is always on).

plugins.description: Reserved — configuring it is currently a hard load error.

EnvironmentsConfig.title: EnvironmentsConfig
EnvironmentsConfig.description: The manifest's ``environments`` block: a default and named diff pairs.

default.description: The environment used when none is given.

DiffPair.title: DiffPair
DiffPair.description: A named baseline/candidate pairing the Diff screen and ``--pair`` resolve.

baseline.description: The baseline environment name or id.

candidate.description: The candidate environment name or id.

RunConfig.title: RunConfig
RunConfig.description: Run-wide execution defaults: concurrency and retry.

backoff.description: How the delay grows between attempts.

SelectionConfig.title: SelectionConfig
SelectionConfig.description: The default request selection for headless ``run``/``diff``.

tags.description: Only run requests carrying any of these tags.

ReportConfig.title: ReportConfig
ReportConfig.description: Report defaults: formats to write, the artifact output dir, archive dir.

formats.description: Reporters to write on a headless run.

dir.description: Directory the replayable report archive is kept in.

retention.description: How many saved reports to keep (newest first); null keeps all.

RedactionConfig.title: RedactionConfig
RedactionConfig.description: Redaction options. The declared-secret display masking is always on.

stringMatchBackstop.description: Forward-compat flag only — the value-match floor is always on and cannot be disabled.
